key_themes: CDP Climate Change and Water Security rating
notable_excerpts: We've had a 5.7% reduction in specific water consumption.
In CDP, we've got a sustained B rating in CDP Climate Change and Water Security themes.
2% increase in women in the factory workforce, which is now allowed even for night shifts with their consent.
Britannia Nutrition Foundation continues to do good work, and there is a 27% increase in the beneficiary through the foundation.
The Britannia Nutrition Foundation work in terms of giving energy dense food material to undernourished children has been recognized as a best CSR project of the year 2025.
We will now run through the financial results, and maybe I ask Venkat to take this through.
If I then take a look at some of the key 5 driving strategic priorities, which we have mentioned.

chain_of_thought: The environmental disclosures focused on water consumption reduction and maintaining a CDP rating. Social aspects highlighted workforce diversity and community nutrition efforts. Governance was less detailed, focusing on financial transparency and strategic priorities.
strengths: Strong commitment to social initiatives, particularly in nutrition and workforce diversity.
Consistent environmental efforts with measurable outcomes.
weaknesses: Governance disclosures lack depth and specificity.
